Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 265 upvotes, 28 comments
  2. OpenBSD on SGI: A Rollercoaster Story 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Arabic document from 17th-cent. rubbish heap confirms semi-legendary Nubian king 46 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide 346 upvotes, 84 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 555 upvotes, 171 comments
  6. “It turns out” (2010) 289 upvotes, 89 comments
  7. Google Workspace CLI 620 upvotes, 207 comments
  8. You Just Reveived 181 upvotes, 53 comments
  9. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 132 upvotes, 45 comments
  10. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 114 upvotes, 39 comments
  11. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 678 upvotes, 301 comments
  12. Poor Man's Polaroid 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  13. Show HN: Poppy – A simple app to stay intentional with relationships 107 upvotes, 42 comments
  14. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 127 upvotes, 53 comments
  15. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 603 upvotes, 320 comments
  16. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 46 upvotes, 16 comments
  17. The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying 235 upvotes, 116 comments
  18. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. Dulce et Decorum Est (1921) 140 upvotes, 76 comments
  20. No right to relicense this project 190 upvotes, 116 comments
  21. Jails for NetBSD – Kernel Enforced Isolation and Native Resource Control 24 upvotes, 9 comments
  22. Aura-State: Formally Verified LLM State Machine Compiler 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  23. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 115 upvotes, 80 comments
  24. What Python’s asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 56 upvotes, 36 comments
  25. Relax NG is a schema language for XML (2014) 28 upvotes, 16 comments
  26. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 88 upvotes, 70 comments
  27. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 147 upvotes, 133 comments
  28. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 144 upvotes, 139 comments
  29. MacBook Neo 1795 upvotes, 2102 comments
  30. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 81 upvotes, 76 comments

Last generated 7:30pm, 5 March 2026. Source here.
